channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: The future of AI isn’t the model—it’s the system
item.description: Welcome to AI Decoded, Fast Company’s weekly newsletter that breaks down the most important news in the world of AI. You can sign up to receive this newsletter every week here.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Samsung Galaxy S26 Ultra could resurrect an intriguing camera feature
item.description: The Samsung Galaxy S26 Ultra has been tipped to feature main camera with physically variable aperture.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Greece says ambitious Mediterranean power cable project is on track despite setbacks
item.description: ATHENS, Greece (AP) — The future of an ambitious electricity cable linking the power grids of Greece, Cyprus and Israel by the end of the decade is on track despite a series of setbacks, Greece’s Foreign Minister Giorgos Gerapetritis said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Meta to start testing crowd-sourced fact-checking, based on X example, next week
item.description: Facebook and Instagram parent company Meta Platforms Inc. said Thursday it will begin testing its crowd-sourced fact-checking program, Community Notes, on March 18. It will initially based on a ratings system used by Elon Musk's X. Meta ended its fact-checking program in January.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Intel reveals its new CEO
item.description: Intel has gained a new CEO three months after Pat Gelsinger announced he’d be leaving – Lip-Bu Tan to take post.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Invincible season 3 ending explained: is [spoiler] dead, Damien Darkblood end credits scene, will there be a season 4, and more big questions answered
item.description: Here's how Invincible season 3 ended on Prime Video, and how it sets up events to come in the Amazon TV show's fourth season.
item.dc:creator: tom.power@futurenet.com (Tom Power), TechRadar

item.title: Deep tech disruption: ecco come le tecnologie avanzate stanno trasformando le aziende
item.description: I CEO, i CIO e i CFO stanno scoprendo che la “deep tech” sta guidando attivamente l’innovazione aziendale e la redditività.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: I’m a tech pro and these are my secrets to better flights and luxury travel
item.description: Here are some travel hacks to save money on your next vacation including setting up price alerts, remaining flexible and checking for 24-hour price drops.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: TikTok’s comment sections are being flooded with copy-pasted Christian messages
item.description: An influx of copy-and-pasted Christian messages has recently taken over TikTok’s comment sections. Over the past several days, comments about Jesus Christ have surfaced among the top comments on a wide range of unrelated videos.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: The Rise of Wearable Tech: RAW Rings Revolutionize Relationship Tracking
item.description: RAW Rings, a new wearable tech device, allows couples to track each other's emotions in real-time. The device uses bio-sensors to detect vital signs and emotional cues, raising concerns about the implications of such technology on relationships.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: More than 10,000 domains registered for smishing campaign: Report
item.description: Cybercriminals have registered more than 10,000 domains for various smishing scams, according to a recent report from the threat intelligence arm at cybersecurity firm Palo Alto Networks.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: How the Trump administration plans to use algorithms to target protesters
item.description: As protests against the Trump administration and in favor of Palestine continue to grow across the country, the U.S. State Department is reportedly planning to use tech to try and tamp down on dissent.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: SAP: Latest news and insights
item.description: SAP is an enterprise software vendor based in Walldorf, Germany. Its cloud and on-premises enterprise resource planning (ERP) software, including S/4HANA, helps organizations manage their business operations and customer relations.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Military AI is here. Some experts are worried
item.description: For the first two-and-a-half years of the generative AI revolution, the AI arms race has been waged between competing companies seeking to make bank from the promise and potential of the technology. But things are maturing in the AI world—and with it, there’s another frontline for AI: the military.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: French publishers and authors sue Meta over copyright works used in AI training
item.description: French publishers and authors said Wednesday they're taking Meta to court, accusing the social media company of using their works without permission to train its artificial intelligence model.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: At long last, OpenStack (now known as OpenInfra Foundation) joins Linux Foundation
item.description: After more than a decade as an independent open-source foundation, the OpenStack project is joining the Linux Foundation in a move aimed at accelerating collaboration across the open infrastructure ecosystem.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Farming Revolution: Carbon Robotics' LaserWeeder G2 Promises Efficient and Greener Weed Control
item.description: Carbon Robotics' latest innovation, the LaserWeeder G2, is set to revolutionize weed control in farming by ditching chemicals altogether.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Google adds Gemini AI image enhancements to Workspace videoconferencing
item.description: Google is jazzing up videoconferencing and chat features in its Workspace suite with new generative AI (genAI) features, including image and background enhancements for Google Meet and built-in translation for Google Chat, the company said on its Workspace update page.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How to deliver more secure capabilities to the warfighter, faster
item.description: Rethinking the delivery process for new capabilities is essential to put cybersecurity and the warfighter front and center. The post How to deliver more secure capabilities to the warfighter, faster first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Daisy Thornton, Federal News Network

item.title: Observe links end-user experience with back-end troubleshooting
item.description: Observe has bolstered its observability platform with frontend monitoring capabilities that it says will enable developers and IT teams to gain visibility into application performance on end-user browsers and mobile applications.
item.dc:creator: Network World
